title: Improve UPI Payment QR Code Scannability with Fallback

requirements.text: On the student-side payment page (frontend/src/pages/student/Book.tsx and any related payment QR rendering component), generate the QR code using a properly formatted UPI deep link in the format: `upi://pay?pa=<UPI_ID>&pn=<PAYEE_NAME>&am=<AMOUNT>&cu=INR`. Ensure this format is used consistently so all UPI-compliant apps (Google Pay, PhonePe, BHIM, Paytm, etc.) can scan and initiate payment without issues.

quotes: its a upi payment only but its not paytm or any other upi app not able to scan it
Proper UPI deep link format — encoding it as upi://pay?pa=YOUR_UPI_ID&pn=NAME&am=AMOUNT&cu=INR so all apps can scan it correctly

acceptanceCriteria: The QR code encodes a valid upi://pay?pa=...&pn=...&am=...&cu=INR URI
Scanning the QR with Google Pay opens a pre-filled payment screen
Scanning the QR with PhonePe opens a pre-filled payment screen
Scanning the QR with BHIM opens a pre-filled payment screen

requirements.text: Upgrade the QR code rendering on the student payment page to use error correction level 'H' (highest, ~30% damage tolerance). Ensure the QR is rendered with sufficient quiet zone (at least 4 modules of white border), high contrast (black modules on white background), and at a resolution high enough for crisp display (minimum 256x256 logical pixels). If using a QR library like 'qrcode.react', set the appropriate props (errorCorrectionLevel='H', size, includeMargin=true).

quotes: High error correction (level H) — makes the QR scannable even if slightly obscured
size is perfect i think some other issues is there

acceptanceCriteria: QR code uses error correction level H
QR is rendered with a white quiet zone border on all sides
QR modules are high contrast black on white
QR is rendered at minimum 256x256 pixels

requirements.text: Below the QR code on the student payment page, add a copyable fallback section that displays: (1) the UPI ID in a styled text field with a one-click copy-to-clipboard button, (2) the full UPI payment deep link as a tappable anchor that opens the user's default UPI app when clicked. Show a brief confirmation toast or inline feedback (e.g., 'Copied!') when the UPI ID is copied. Label this section clearly, e.g., 'Can't scan? Use these instead:'.

quotes: Copyable fallback — a one-tap copy button for the UPI ID and a direct payment link students can open manually

acceptanceCriteria: A clearly labeled fallback section appears below the QR code
The UPI ID is displayed with a copy button that copies it to clipboard
A 'Pay Now' or 'Open Payment App' link is shown that opens the upi:// deep link
Clicking copy shows a 'Copied!' confirmation message
The fallback section is visible on both mobile and desktop

constraints: Do not change the backend schema or any Motoko backend logic
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Preserve existing payment flow and form validation logic
nonGoals: Changing the payment amount logic or course fee calculations
Adding new payment methods beyond UPI
Backend changes to payment records or storage
Redesigning the overall payment page layout
